---
title: "Biking Weather Forecast"
subtitle: "Multi-model ride planning"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-location: left
    toc-title: "On this page"
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
    fig-align: left
    code-tools: true
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    documentclass: scrartcl
    geometry: margin=1in
    fig-align: center
    fig-pos: "H"
    colorlinks: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
jupyter: python3
---

<!--
Render HTML:  quarto render dc_bike_weather_report.qmd --to html
Render PDF:   quarto render dc_bike_weather_report.qmd --to pdf
Both:         quarto render dc_bike_weather_report.qmd

Requirements: pip install jupyter requests pandas numpy matplotlib
PDF also needs LaTeX:  quarto install tinytex   (one time)
Keep this .qmd next to dc_bike_weather.py (imported as the analysis engine).
The setup chunk fetches live data from Open-Meteo + NWS, so it needs internet.

Every knob lives in the `parameters` cell below and can be overridden at render
time WITHOUT editing this file:

  Single values on the command line:
    quarto render dc_bike_weather_report.qmd -P units:metric -P forecast_days:1

  Lists / several values via a YAML file (params.yml):
    location_name: "Boulder, CO"
    lat: 40.0150
    lon: -105.2705
    tz: "America/Denver"
    units: metric
    forecast_days: 3
    preferred_hours: [6, 10]
    models: [gfs_seamless, ecmwf_ifs025, icon_seamless, ecmwf_aifs025]
  then:
    quarto render dc_bike_weather_report.qmd --execute-params params.yml

  On Posit Connect Cloud (which has no -P UI) set environment variables instead:
    Advanced settings -> Configure variables -> Add variable   (at publish time),
    or later from the content's Settings page. Use the BIKE_* names listed in the
    cell below, e.g.  BIKE_UNITS = metric ,  BIKE_LOCATION_NAME = Boulder, CO ,
    BIKE_MODELS = gfs_seamless,ecmwf_ifs025  (comma-separated; "none" = all models).
    Precedence (later wins): defaults here  <  BIKE_* env vars  <  -P / params file.
-->

```{python}
#| label: parameters
#| tags: [parameters]
# Defaults below describe Washington, DC. They can be overridden two ways, in
# order of precedence (later wins): BIKE_* environment variables (how Posit
# Connect Cloud injects values), then -P / --execute-params at render time.
import os, json

def _env(k, d):                 # string
    v = os.getenv(k); return v if v not in (None, "") else d
def _envf(k, d):                # float
    v = os.getenv(k); return float(v) if v not in (None, "") else d
def _envi(k, d):                # int
    v = os.getenv(k); return int(v) if v not in (None, "") else d
def _envb(k, d):                # bool: 1/true/yes/on -> True
    v = os.getenv(k)
    return d if v in (None, "") else v.strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
def _envlist(k, d):             # "a,b,c" or JSON list, or "none" -> None
    v = os.getenv(k)
    if v in (None, ""): return d
    v = v.strip()
    if v.lower() == "none": return None
    return json.loads(v) if v.startswith("[") else [x.strip() for x in v.split(",") if x.strip()]

location_name = _env("BIKE_LOCATION_NAME", "Washington, DC")
lat  = _envf("BIKE_LAT", 38.9072)
lon  = _envf("BIKE_LON", -77.0369)
tz   = _env("BIKE_TZ", "America/New_York")
forecast_days = _envi("BIKE_FORECAST_DAYS", 2)    # 1 = today only; 2 = today + tomorrow
units = _env("BIKE_UNITS", "us")                  # "us" -> degF, mph ; "metric" -> degC, km/h
have_light = _envb("BIKE_HAVE_LIGHT", True)        # dark riding ok -> small night penalty only
ride_hours = _envi("BIKE_RIDE_HOURS", 3)          # length of the headline "best ride window"
show_alerts = _envb("BIKE_SHOW_ALERTS", True)     # include active NWS alerts
alert_email = _env("BIKE_ALERT_EMAIL", "replace-with-your-email@example.com")

_ph = _envlist("BIKE_PREFERRED_HOURS", [5, 9])    # "5,9" or "none"; [start, end) no-dark window
preferred_hours = [int(x) for x in _ph] if _ph else None
models = _envlist("BIKE_MODELS", None)            # "gfs_seamless,ecmwf_ifs025"; none/"" = all

mode = _env("BIKE_MODE", "both")                  # "bike", "row", or "both"
usgs_site = _env("BIKE_USGS_SITE", "01646500")    # USGS gauge for river conditions (rowing)
_wt = _env("BIKE_WATER_TEMP_C", "")               # manual water temp °C, if the gauge lacks it
water_temp_c = float(_wt) if _wt else None
```

```{python}
#| label: setup
import os, matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np, pandas as pd
import dc_bike_weather as bw

# Push the parameters from the cell above into the analysis engine. This sets
# location, units, rider profile, model set, etc. and recomputes derived values.
bw.configure(
    location_name=location_name, lat=lat, lon=lon, tz=tz,
    forecast_days=forecast_days, units=units, have_light=have_light,
    preferred_hours=preferred_hours, ride_hours=ride_hours, models=models,
    show_alerts=show_alerts, alert_email=alert_email,
    mode=mode, usgs_site=usgs_site, water_temp_c=water_temp_c,
)

FIGDIR = "report_figs"
os.makedirs(FIGDIR, exist_ok=True)

# One live fetch shared by the whole report.
js = bw.fetch()
panel = bw.to_panel(js)
alerts = bw.fetch_alerts() if show_alerts else []
n_models = len(panel)

# Which views to render, and the live river conditions for the rowing view.
bike_on = mode in ("bike", "both")
row_on = mode in ("row", "both")
river = bw.fetch_river(usgs_site) if row_on else None
if row_on:
    bw.add_row_scores(panel, (river or {}).get("water_c"))

# Split into per-day frames AFTER rowing scores are attached, so each day carries them.
days = bw.split_by_day(panel)


def table_html(idx, agg):
    """Consensus-by-hour table as HTML, with the rating cell colour-coded."""
    head, cells, rate_idx = bw.consensus_rows(idx, agg)
    th = "".join(f"<th style='padding:3px 9px'>{h}</th>" for h in head)
    rows = []
    for row, ri in zip(cells, rate_idx):
        tds = "".join(
            f"<td style='padding:3px 9px;text-align:center'>{v}</td>" for v in row[:-1])
        col = bw.RATING_COLORS[ri]
        tds += (f"<td style='padding:3px 9px;text-align:center;background:{col};"
                f"color:#111;font-weight:600'>{row[-1]}</td>")
        rows.append(f"<tr>{tds}</tr>")
    return ("<table style='border-collapse:collapse;font-size:0.85em;margin:0.4em 0'>"
            f"<thead><tr style='background:#333;color:#fff'>{th}</tr></thead>"
            f"<tbody>{''.join(rows)}</tbody></table>")


def save(fig, name):
    path = os.path.join(FIGDIR, name)
    fig.savefig(path, dpi=110, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close(fig)
    return path
```

```{python}
#| label: intro
#| output: asis
units = f"{bw.TU}, {bw.WU}"
day_names = " and ".join(bw.day_label(d).split(" \u00b7 ")[0].lower() for d, _ in days)
profile = []
if bw.HAVE_LIGHT:
    profile.append("you ride with a powerful light")
if bw.PREFERRED_HOURS:
    profile.append(f"you often ride {bw.PREFERRED_HOURS[0]}:00\u2013{bw.PREFERRED_HOURS[1]}:00")
prof = (" Recommendations assume " + " and ".join(profile) + ".") if profile else ""

print(f"Hour-by-hour cycling conditions for **{location_name}**, comparing "
      f"**{n_models} weather models** (physics + AI) for **{day_names}**. "
      f"Units are {units}.{prof} Each day shows an at-a-glance suitability grid, "
      f"the recommended ride window, a consensus table, and detailed temperature, "
      f"storm and wind charts \u2014 plus a heat-stress (WBGT) or wind-chill panel "
      f"on days when heat or cold is a concern.\n")
```

```{python}
#| label: alerts
#| output: asis
if alerts:
    rank = {"Extreme": 0, "Severe": 1, "Moderate": 2, "Minor": 3, "Unknown": 4}
    ordered = sorted(alerts, key=lambda a: rank.get(a["severity"], 5))
    kind = "important" if ordered[0]["severity"] in ("Extreme", "Severe") else "warning"
    print(f"::: {{.callout-{kind} title='Active NWS alerts'}}")
    for a in ordered:
        print(f"- **{a['event']}** ({a['severity']}) \u2014 "
              f"{bw.fmt_alert_window(a['onset'], a['ends'])}")
    print(":::\n")
```

```{python}
#| label: river
#| output: asis
# Live USGS river conditions (rowing only) — shown once; this is current, not a forecast.
if row_on:
    rn = bw.river_notes(river)
    if rn:
        kind, title, lines = rn
        print(f"::: {{.callout-{kind} title='{title}'}}")
        for ln in lines:
            print(f"- {ln}")
        print(":::\n")
```

```{python}
#| label: days
#| output: asis
# Each day is a tab in the HTML build. In PDF a tabset has no meaning, so Quarto
# renders the tabs as a sequence of sections instead — one day per page below.
print("::: {.panel-tabset}\n")
for di_, (d, sub) in enumerate(days):
    didx = sub[next(iter(sub))].index
    sun = bw.sun_times(bw.LAT, bw.LON, d)
    agg = bw.build_aggregates(sub)
    bullets, win = bw.summarize(sub, didx, agg, sun)
    label = bw.day_label(d)

    if di_ > 0:                       # PDF only: each day starts on a new page
        print('::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}')
        print("{{< pagebreak >}}")
        print(":::\n")
    print(f"\n## {label}\n")

    both_views = bike_on and row_on
    if both_views:                    # nested tabset: Cycling / Rowing tabs in HTML
        print("::: {.panel-tabset}\n")
        print("### Cycling\n")
    if bike_on:
        # Headline recommendation (first bullet, minus the leading star).
        print("::: {.callout-tip title='Recommended ride window'}")
        print(bullets[0].replace("\u2605 ", "") + "\n")
        print(":::\n")

        # At-a-glance suitability grid.
        p = save(bw.fig_heatmap(sub, didx, agg, sun, win), f"heat_{di_}.png")
        print(f"![Hourly bikeability \u2014 each model vs the consensus]({p}){{width=100%}}\n")

        # Explain any hours a model rates Poor or worse, and whether models agree.
        notes = bw.low_rating_notes(sub, didx)
        if notes:
            print("::: {.callout-note title='Why some hours rate low'}")
            for n in notes:
                print(f"- {n}")
            print(":::\n")

        # Narrative.
        print("**What the models say**\n")
        for b in bullets[1:]:
            print(f"- {b}")
        print()

        # Consensus table: rich HTML for the web build, colour-coded image for PDF.
        print("**Consensus every 3 hours**\n")
        print('::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}')
        print(table_html(didx, agg))
        print(":::\n")
        tp = save(bw.fig_table(didx, agg), f"table_{di_}.png")
        print('::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}')
        print(f"![]({tp}){{width=85%}}")
        print(":::\n")

        # Heat-stress (WBGT) view — shown only when heat is a concern.
        hf = bw.heat_flags(didx, agg)
        if hf["concern"]:
            flag = hf["peak_flag"]
            kind = "important" if flag in ("Red", "Black") else "warning"
            wins = hf["windows"]
            span = (f"; in the {flag} zone {wins[flag]}"
                    if wins.get(flag) else "")
            print(f"::: {{.callout-{kind} title='Heat stress \u2014 {flag} flag'}}")
            print(f"Peak WBGT **{bw.Td(hf['peak_wbgt_c']):.0f}{bw.TU}** ({flag}) "
                  f"around {bw.hm(hf['peak_hour'])}{span}.\n")
            if hf["wetbulb_danger"]:
                print(f"- **Wet-bulb reaches {bw.Td(hf['wetbulb_max_c']):.0f}{bw.TU}** "
                      f"\u2014 sweat can barely cool you; treat any hard effort as dangerous.")
            for r in hf["recommendations"]:
                print(f"- {r}")
            print(":::\n")
            pw = save(bw.fig_wbgt(sub, didx, agg, label, sun), f"wbgt_{di_}.png")
            print(f"![Heat stress (WBGT) against athletic flags]({pw}){{width=100%}}\n")

        # Wind-chill view — shown only when cold is a concern.
        cf = bw.cold_flags(didx, agg)
        if cf["concern"]:
            band = cf["band"]
            kind = ("important" if band in ("Very cold", "Dangerous")
                    else "warning" if band == "Cold" else "note")
            print(f"::: {{.callout-{kind} title='Wind chill \u2014 {band}'}}")
            print(f"Feels-like low **{bw.Td(cf['min_wc_c']):.0f}{bw.TU}** ({band}) "
                  f"around {bw.hm(cf['min_hour'])}; wind makes it up to "
                  f"{bw.dTd(cf['max_gap_c']):.0f}{bw.TU} colder than the air.\n")
            for r in cf["recommendations"]:
                print(f"- {r}")
            print(":::\n")
            pwc = save(bw.fig_windchill(sub, didx, agg, label, sun), f"wchill_{di_}.png")
            print(f"![Wind chill (feels-like) against frostbite risk]({pwc}){{width=100%}}\n")

        # Detailed charts.
        pt = save(bw.fig_thermal(sub, didx, agg, label, sun), f"therm_{di_}.png")
        print(f"![Temperature, heat stress and UV]({pt}){{width=100%}}\n")
        pc = save(bw.fig_conditions(sub, didx, agg, label, sun), f"cond_{di_}.png")
        print(f"![Precipitation, storms and wind]({pc}){{width=100%}}\n")

    if row_on:
        if both_views:
            print("### Rowing\n")
        rp = save(bw.fig_heatmap(sub, didx, agg, sun, None, score_col="row_score",
                                 title="Hourly rowing conditions \u2014 each model vs the consensus"),
                  f"row_{di_}.png")
        print(f"![Hourly rowing conditions \u2014 each model vs the consensus]({rp}){{width=100%}}\n")
        rnotes = bw.row_low_rating_notes(sub, didx, (river or {}).get("water_c"))
        if rnotes:
            print("::: {.callout-note title='Why some rowing hours rate low'}")
            for n in rnotes:
                print(f"- {n}")
            print(":::\n")

    if both_views:
        print(":::\n")                # close the nested Cycling/Rowing tabset

print(":::\n")                        # close the day tabset
```

## Methodology & data

```{python}
#| label: method
#| output: asis
print(bw.methodology_text() + "\n")
if row_on:
    print("\n" + bw.row_methodology_text() + "\n")
print("\n*" + bw.methodology_src() + "*")
```
